description.content: Invoke PostgreSQL functions via remote procedure calls.
Calls a PostgreSQL function (stored procedure) via a remote procedure call. Function arguments are passed in the request body. Functions must be created in the public schema or an exposed schema.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
CRUD operations on database tables. Paths are auto-generated based on your database schema.

Retrieves rows from a database table with support for filtering, sorting, pagination, and column selection. PostgREST operators are used in query parameters for horizontal filtering (e.g. eq, neq, gt, lt, gte, lte, like, ilike, in, is, fts, plfts, phfts, wfts, cs, cd, ov, sl, sr, nxl, nxr, adj, not, or, and). Vertical filtering uses the select parameter.

query.description: Comma-separated list of columns to return. Supports renaming (alias:column), casting (column::type), and embedding related resources via foreign keys (related_table(columns)).
Comma-separated list of columns to sort by. Append .desc for descending, .asc for ascending. Use .nullsfirst or .nullslast to control null ordering.
Maximum number of rows to return
Number of rows to skip before starting to return rows

variable.description: (Required) Name of the database table or view
Your Supabase project reference ID

header.description: Request a specific range of rows using HTTP Range header (e.g. 0-24 for first 25 rows).
Request a total row count. Use count=exact for precise count, count=planned for estimated count, or count=estimated for a combination.

description.content: Inserts one or more rows into a database table. Use the Prefer header with return=representation to get the inserted rows in the response. Supports upsert with Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates.

description.content: Your Supabase project reference ID

The Supabase Database REST API provides auto-generated RESTful endpoints for every table, view, and function in your PostgreSQL database. Powered by PostgREST, it supports full CRUD operations, complex filtering with horizontal and vertical filtering operators, pagination, sorting, and embedding of related resources via foreign key relationships. Row Level Security policies are enforced on all API requests. The schema is auto-generated from your database, so paths and schemas vary per project. This specification documents the common patterns and conventions.
